_meta.description: MobSF manifest-level baseline findings for OVAA. Purposely vulnerable Android app by Oversecured for security scanner testing.

mobsf_findings.issue_title: Application Data can be Backed Up
mobsf_findings.description: android:allowBackup=true allows application data to be backed up via adb.

mobsf_findings.issue_title: Clear Text Traffic Is Enabled For App
mobsf_findings.description: android:usesCleartextTraffic=true explicitly enables cleartext HTTP traffic.

mobsf_findings.issue_title: App can be installed on older vulnerable Android versions
mobsf_findings.description: minSdkVersion 23 (Android 6.0 Marshmallow) is outdated (< API 24/Nougat) but not critically so (>= API 19).
Multiple components exported without permission protection: 9 activities, 2 content providers, and 1 broadcast receiver all exported with android:exported=true and no permission requirement.
mobsf_findings.evidence_pattern: 9 exported activities, 2 exported providers, 1 exported receiver

mobsf_findings.description: Activity with singleTask launchMode is vulnerable to StrandHogg 2.0 task hijacking attacks. TheftActivity uses launchMode=singleTask.
Multiple browsable activities handle deep links: DeeplinkActivity handles custom scheme oversecured://, WebViewActivity handles https://oversecured.com without autoVerify.
mobsf_findings.evidence_pattern: oversecured:// custom scheme, https://oversecured.com without autoVerify

mobsf_findings.description: DeeplinkActivity uses a custom URL scheme (oversecured://) which cannot be verified via Digital Asset Links and can be registered by any app.

expected_absent.reason: OVAA is a release build (android:debuggable not set)

summary.notes: MobSF baseline for OVAA produces 6-7 manifest findings. MANIFEST-010 may produce 1-2 sub-findings (custom scheme vs unverified https). All findings are true positives since OVAA is intentionally vulnerable.
